steps.title: Identify the relationship between heights
steps.content:
The plant grows at a constant average rate over 12 days. The final height equals the initial height plus all the growth that occurred:

$$\text{Final height} = \text{Initial height} + \text{Total growth}$$

steps.title: Calculate the total growth
steps.content:
Growth occurs at 1.20 cm per day for 12 days:
$$\text{Total growth} = 1.20 \times 12 = 14.4 \text{ cm}$$

steps.title: Solve for initial height
steps.content:
Substitute into the relationship:
$$36.8 = \text{Initial height} + 14.4$$
$$\text{Initial height} = 36.8 - 14.4 = 22.4 \text{ cm}$$
